element.definition: Server-side state for one SMART Health Link (SHL): the encryption key, the encrypted payload, and the protocol bookkeeping (passcode, expiry, throttling, file tokens) needed to resolve it.

The base64url-encoded 256-bit AES key that encrypts this link's file. It also travels inside the shlink: URI, so only the holder of the link can decrypt the result. The server stores it here only so the background worker can encrypt the result once the job finishes.
Short description of what the link carries, shown to the receiver (e.g. "Eligibility result for Jane Prospect").

Lifecycle of the content-producing job. 'pending' while the (long-running) job is in flight — the manifest reports 'can-change'; 'ready' once the encrypted file is attached — the manifest reports 'finalized'.

element.short: MIME type of the decrypted file
element.definition: Content type of the payload after decryption, reported in the manifest (e.g. 'application/fhir+json' for a CoverageEligibilityResponse).

element.short: Reference to the source FHIR resource
element.definition: Reference to the FHIR resource the encrypted content was produced from (e.g. 'CoverageEligibilityResponse/123'), for traceability.

The link's content encrypted as a JWE compact serialization (alg 'dir', enc 'A256GCM') under SHLink.key. This is the only form of the content the server stores or serves; it is set when the job becomes 'ready'.
Optional absolute expiry as a Unix timestamp in seconds. After this moment the link resolves to 'no-longer-valid' and file fetches return 410 Gone.
When set, the link carries the 'P' flag and the manifest requires this passcode (delivered out-of-band, never inside the shlink:) to resolve. A second secret guarding against link interception.
Lifetime count of incorrect-passcode attempts left before the link locks permanently. Decremented (and persisted) before responding, so parallel guesses cannot bypass the limit.
Timestamp of the most recent successful manifest poll, in milliseconds. Used to throttle frequent polling of the same link (429 + Retry-After).
Tokens minted when the manifest returns a 'location' URL (instead of embedding the file). Each grants a short, time-bounded fetch of the encrypted file.
The token value, of the form '<shlId>~<random>', embedded in the manifest's 'location' URL so the file endpoint can resolve the link without a custom search parameter.
Absolute expiry of the file token in milliseconds. After it, fetching the location returns 410 Gone.
